title: Progress Test
objectives: Assess travel and world vocabulary from Unit 5
Test reading comprehension about holiday planning
Evaluate grammar: present perfect with just and prepositions of time
Assess spelling: -tion suffix and travel vocabulary
Write a structured paragraph using a graphic organiser

exercises.problem: The plane will ............ in Kuwait in ten minutes. (a. land, b. greet, c. discuss, d. realise)
I followed the ............ to finish my homework. (a. continents, b. seats, c. flights, d. instructions)
There are ............ tasty cupcakes on the table. (a. monsoon, b. historical, c. few, d. dangerous)
The children can play ............ in the park. (a. fluently, b. freely, c. indoors, d. tightly)
Read about Omar's family discussing holiday ideas. The best title: (a. The Museum, b. Library Time, c. The Nature, d. Holiday Ideas)
The underlined word 'quiet' in line 3 means: (a. calm, b. happy, c. new, d. funny)
The underlined word 'he' in line 8 refers to: (a. Omar, b. Ali, c. father, d. grandfather)
Omar wants to read a book about: (a. science, b. museum, c. library, d. history)
What did Ali want to read about?
exercises.answer: Ali wanted to visit the museum and read about history.

exercises.problem: Why do you think Omar felt happy at the end of the passage?
exercises.answer: Because everyone shared something interesting and he was glad to hear new ideas.

exercises.problem: We have an English class ............ Wednesday. (a. at, b. on, c. in)
She has just ............ her project. (a. finished, b. finish, c. finishing)
exercises.answer: b. finish (has just + base form of past participle, but 'finished' is the past participle — answer is a. finished)

exercises.problem: The match starts ............ eight o'clock. (a. in, b. at, c. on)
They ............ just built a new museum. (a. has, b. are, c. have)
1. The computer's ............ is bright. 2. The ............ season is full of heavy rains.
Fill in graphic organiser about 'Grandfather's Arrival' using guide words: airport-family-wait-few-shops-late. Answer: Where? Who? What? What did you see? When back? Then write a paragraph.
exercises.answer: Yesterday, I went to the airport with my family. We waited for grandfather's flight. We saw a few shops. The plane was late. We went back home in the evening.

teaching_notes: This progress test is 4 pages — more comprehensive than Grade 3 tests. Vocabulary section uses 4 options (a-d). Reading passage is about family discussion — tests inference ('Why do you think Omar felt happy?'). Grammar tests both present perfect and prepositions. Writing uses a graphic organiser with 5 guiding questions — more structured than Grade 3.
